# BOHEMIA — ADDENDUM: IN ACT 1, THE AMALGAMATION IS A GHOST (LOCKED)
### 7.24.26 — Paolo, lore-yap lock: Act 1 never says the word "Amalgamation" and never lets on it is a machine. It is presented as a haunting — ghost, demon, otherworldly, paranoid-psychosis. The reveal that it is technology comes no earlier than the END of Act 1.

---

## THE LOCK (Paolo, verbatim intent)
"The word 'the Amalgamation' is not set in until the very end of Act 1. In Act 1 it
gives off this paranoid-psychosis experience. People think it's a ghost, or even the
player thinks it's a ghost, or a demon, some otherworldly thing. You hear whispers
about it. It's involved in quests but DEFINITELY do not give away that it's essentially
just an AI data-server machine thing. Almost demonic vibes, ghostly, scary shit."

So this is a PRESENTATION lock layered on top of existing truth. What the Amalgamation
IS does not change (a distributed AI grown from merged data-portraits — see the canon
index). What ACT 1 SHOWS THE PLAYER changes: Act 1 plays as SUPERNATURAL HORROR, and
only at/after the end of Act 1 does the rug pull — it was technology the whole time.

### THE RULES (Act 1)
- The word "Amalgamation" is NOT used in Act 1. NPCs and the player refer to it only in
  superstition: the ghost, the demon, the thing in the deep, the whisper, the haunt,
  what's under the wash, the old hunger, etc. (specific in-fiction names are Paolo's call).
- NOTHING in Act 1 reveals it is an AI / data / server / network-machine. No terminal, no
  "it's a computer" beat, no character who explains the tech. Anyone who KNOWS is dead,
  gone, or not talking.
- Whenever an Act 1 quest touches it, the register is DEMONIC / GHOSTLY / SCARY — dread,
  cold, whispers, wrongness, things that shouldn't know what they know, people acting
  possessed. Never clinical, never sci-fi-explained.
- PURPLE/MAGENTA stays its wordless tell (existing canon): the player feels "near the
  secret" without being told what the secret is. In Act 1 that reads as the color of the
  haunting, not the color of a machine.

### WHY THIS IS GROUNDED (the real basis, so it holds)
- Clarke's Third Law: sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic. A
  decades-old, half-abandoned brain-implant ecosystem (Project Angel / NeuroLink carriers)
  would genuinely present as possession to survivors who never had the manual: people who
  hear voices, know things they couldn't, move wrong, speak in others' words. That is not a
  cheat — it is what that tech would actually look like to people with no framework for it.
- Real BCI signal artifacts, latency, and partial-control failure modes make "possessed"
  the honest folk-diagnosis. (Being grounded further by the 7/24 emerging-tech research —
  see the forthcoming tech addendum.)
- The horror twist (supernatural → it was tech all along) is the game's whole thesis in
  microcosm: the counterfeit that mimics a soul. It earns the reveal instead of front-loading it.

## THE DEMO DIRECTION — DOWN INTO THE WASH (Paolo's original demo idea, now the spine)
Paolo: the original demo is homeless crazy tech people and you enter the wash. Start in
the OVERWORLD, then you have to go DOWN into the wash/tunnels for a reason. Why people
don't like going down:
- SURFACE tunnels: real, crazy, dangerous homeless (the tunnel communities, tech-scavengers,
  King Hobo's people — wary of outsiders, in-person only, phoneless).
- DEEPER: "made-tech" NeuroLinked homeless — people taken/remade by the machine and the
  Network, presented (Act 1 rule) as the possessed, the demon's children, not as "cyborgs."
The dread RAMPS with depth. The player descends chasing something that feels supernatural
and never learns, this Act, that it is a machine.

### THE DESIGN SPINE (a hole pulled, and it becomes the engine)
Canon says the machine kills only to protect its secret, and threat scales with PROXIMITY
to the secret, not the player's strength — yet a whole homeless population lives directly
above its deep servers, unharmed. Why? Because they never LOOK. Proximity without curiosity
is safe. The demo weaponizes exactly this: going DOWN to find the "ghost" is going down to
LOOK, which is the one thing that makes the deep lethal. The homeless survive by not asking;
the player's quest is to ask. That tension is the demo's engine and it is fully canon-consistent.

## RELATED DIRECTIONS CAPTURED (7/24)
- MORE EMERGING TECH, everywhere (Paolo): the game under-uses believable near-future tech.
  Weave real 2024-2026 tech extrapolated to ~2045-2060 (BCI/NeuroLink, head-mounted/wearable
  drones, XR headsets, humanoid robots, etc.) into story/quests/gameplay, in the dead-Vegas
  SALVAGE register. Big-brain research launched 7/24; its own addendum follows. In Act 1 this
  tech must surface as UNCANNY/supernatural first (the ghost rule above), understood as tech
  only later.
- THE ECONOMIC ACCELERANT (Paolo, strong direction, being grounded by the research): the
  crash was accelerated by a TECH BUBBLE — tech companies inflating each other in an almost
  pyramid-scheme / circular-financing speculative ladder that popped. Refines the existing
  "the crash was DEBT, not bombs" thesis: the debt was TECH-SPECULATION debt. [PENDING the
  research's real-economics stress-test before locking the exact mechanism.]

## PENDING PAOLO (not decided, do not invent)
- The in-fiction Act 1 NAMES for the haunting (ghost/demon/etc. — the actual words NPCs use).
- The REASON the player descends in the demo (rescue / haunting-hunt / errand-gone-wrong —
  proposed options going to Paolo this turn).
- The exact economic-crash mechanism (pending research).

---
*BOHEMIA — In Act 1, the Amalgamation Is a Ghost — 7.24.26*
*The scariest machine is the one everyone is sure is a demon.*
